description: Wave 12 - 'Operate the Benchmark at Scale: Resumable Campaigns, Hard Budgets, Trustworthy Results' (DRAFT, not run). Padrino is backend-mature; Wave 11 (US-224..243) surfaced the product. This wave makes the benchmark OPERABLE at scale on a SINGLE HOST with a DB-as-queue (FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED; NO broker/Redis), determinism-preserving and firewall-respecting. A CAMPAIGN is the scientific unit; a GAUNTLET is the atomic scheduling unit; this wave REUSES gauntlets/ + runner/scheduler.py rather than replacing them. Alembic head is 0051 (down chain 0049->0050->0051); new migrations are a single linear chain 0052 (per-game lease) -> 0053 (campaigns/pairings) -> 0054 (budget reservation slot). LOCKED product decisions: (1) large field 20+ models -> a COVERAGE-GUARANTEED SUBSET pairing generator (NOT exhaustive round-robin), each model meets enough distinct opponents with balanced faction/role exposure to hit a per-model game target (~50 GLOBAL + ~50/faction); SWISS/rating-adaptive pairing is DEFERRED; gauntlets/games are materialized INCREMENTALLY from a persisted pairing-matrix ledger, not thousands of rows up front; (2) budget = GLOBAL cap + PER-CAMPAIGN cap enforced by an ATOMIC finite-slot reservation generalizing economics/human_cost_governance._claim_inference_slot (unique constraint + begin_nested + fail-closed); NO per-model $ sub-budgets; (3) concurrency = ONE worker draining the campaign near-term, but a crash-safe game-grain claim + lease/fence is still built so a restart resumes and a reaped-then-resurrected worker cannot double-finalize (two-worker stress is NOT a gate); (4) publish gate = UNCERTAINTY-based (per-scope games>=target AND sigma<=target AND non-provisional AND rank-stable over last K updates); an exhausted pairing cell is DEAD_LETTERED and the report DOCUMENTS the hole; the campaign AUTO-FINALIZES (no hard operator block); (5) cost-trust = derived per-call cost (LiteLLM response_cost + token-price fallback) made IMMUTABLE by stamping the price-table version / priced-at-call-time on LlmCall so changing fallback rates never rewrites history; provider-invoice reconciliation DEFERRED. Four phases. P1 (resilience backbone, HARD prereq for P3): benchmark (AI-only) games currently have NO resume - run_game accepts no resume param and the scheduler executor calls run_game(...) with no resume, so any re-drive past GameCreated restarts at sequence 0 and collides on uq_game_event_sequence; the 'retry up to game_max_attempts' is a no-op for real crashes. P1 adds benchmark rehydration mirroring runner/human_durability, wires resume into run_game + the scheduler executor, adds Alembic 0052 per-game lease columns + a game-grain claim + a stale-game reaper run as a CONTINUOUS gated tick, a lease-fence re-check inside the finalize transaction, retryable-vs-poison exception classification at the retry seam, the wall-clock-seed reproducibility fix (gauntlet_job uses f'scheduled:{schedule_id}:{now.isoformat()}'), and a CI replay test proving an interrupted+resumed game yields a byte-identical final event_hash. P2 (pure-core, independent): a deterministic core/scheduling/pairing.py coverage-guaranteed-subset generator with guaranteed (not in-expectation) faction/role-family balance, campaign-seed derivation sha256(b'campaign:'+seed+cell_index), property tests + a purity-firewall test. P3 (DEPENDS on P1+P2): Alembic 0053 campaigns + campaign_pairings + nullable gauntlets.campaign_id + campaign owner/lease columns; a campaign_repo; INCREMENTAL materialization of child gauntlets/games from the ledger; dead-letter after bounded retries; a campaign TickHook composing with run_scheduler; TIGHTENED completion semantics (a gauntlet/campaign is COMPLETED only when every child is terminal-COMPLETED-or-DEAD_LETTER and faction/role balance is within tolerance), fixing the loose _drive_gauntlet that marks COMPLETED despite FAILED children. P4 (budget independent-ish; campaign-scoped + publish-gate parts depend on P3): a shared ATOMIC budget-reservation module (Alembic 0054 slot table modeled on HumanInferenceReservation) wired into the BENCHMARK admission path enforcing global + per-campaign caps as a hard halt (never kill an active game; admit at game grain); fix economics/admission.py to treat FAILED as non-active (it gates on status!='COMPLETED' today); stamp an immutable price-table-version/priced-at on LlmCall; a Campaign report (cost/progress/ETA/per-scope convergence) via CLI/JSON + Prometheus gauges; budget-burn + cost-drift alert keys; and a PUBLISH-GATE checklist returning a 'ready to publish' boolean + blockers list. Hard rules retained: pure core (no wall-clock/unseeded-random/DB/secrets in core/; SeededRng; clocks/leases injected impure), hash-chain + replay determinism, ruleset-by-id, the CANONICAL rating firewall (the canonical CANONICAL_TEAM ladder must never receive non-canonical/PLACEMENT results), human/scientific segregation. OUT OF SCOPE (do NOT add stories): SWISS/rating-adaptive pairing; multi-host/distributed/Redis; provider-invoice reconciliation; per-model $ sub-budgets; intra-game hard cost kill; cron->campaign recurring scheduling; open/BYO model submission; a frontend campaign UI (CLI/API first). DRAFT: 21 stories. Not activated, not run.

userStories.title: Benchmark game rehydration (replay-from-events for AI-only in-progress games)
userStories.description: The human lane already has durable rehydration: runner/human_durability.rehydrate_active_human_games() lists runtime rows, fetches each Game, queries its GameEvent rows in sequence order, and calls replay_state_from_rows(rows) (which runs replay_event_log to verify the hash chain then folds each body through apply_event) to rebuild (GameState, EventLog). Benchmark (AI-only) games have NO such path - they have no human_game_runtime row, so a crashed in-progress benchmark game can only be rebuilt by reading its persisted game_events. Add a benchmark rehydration helper that, given a benchmark game_id, loads its GameEvent rows and rebuilds a GameResume (state, event_log, phase, deadline_at, buffer_snapshot) using the same replay_state_from_rows core that the human lane uses, returning None when the game is already terminal or has no events past GameCreated. This is the foundation that US-250 wires into the scheduler. REUSE replay_state_from_rows and the human_durability template; do not duplicate replay logic.
acceptanceCriteria: A benchmark rehydration helper (e.g. runner/benchmark_durability.rehydrate_benchmark_game(session, game_id) -> GameResume | None) loads the game's GameEvent rows in sequence order and rebuilds (GameState, EventLog) via the shared replay_state_from_rows (the same core the human lane uses), then derives the resume phase from the rebuilt state (reuse the _resume_phase logic / format_phase_id), returning a GameResume whose state/event_log/phase reflect the persisted tail.

It returns None for a game with no events, a game whose only event is GameCreated (resume not needed - it can restart cleanly), and a game whose state.terminal_result is not None (already terminal) - a test asserts each None case and one populated-resume case.

The rebuilt EventLog passes the hash-chain verification (replay_event_log raises ReplayHashMismatchError on tamper) - a test asserts a tampered event row makes rehydration raise rather than silently resume a corrupt chain.
Pure-replay only: the helper reads GameEvent rows via a repository and does NO wall-clock/unseeded-random; the GameState is rebuilt only by replaying events (never mutated directly).
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Template: runner/human_durability.rehydrate_active_human_games (lines ~137-192) + replay_state_from_rows (~107-128). GameResume fields (game_runner.py ~113-128): state, event_log, phase, deadline_at, buffer_snapshot. _resume_phase at game_runner.py ~681-688. events repo: db/repositories/events.list_events / persisted_sequences_from. Benchmark games have NO human_game_runtime row, so rehydration must read game_events directly. This is the #1 fix foundation; US-250 wires it into the scheduler.

userStories.title: Wire GameResume into run_game and the scheduler executor (benchmark games resume past GameCreated)
userStories.description: THE #1 FIX. run_game(config, adapter, ranked, *, persistence=None) (game_runner.py ~1272) does NOT accept a resume parameter; drive_game_loop already does (resume: GameResume | None = None, ~1019) but run_game never threads it. The scheduler's _default_game_executor (scheduler.py ~85-91) calls run_game(config, adapter, ranked, persistence=persistence) with NO resume, so every benchmark re-drive restarts at sequence 0 and emits a second GameCreated, colliding on uq_game_event_sequence (game_id, sequence) - making _run_with_retry's 'retry up to game_max_attempts' a no-op for real mid-game crashes. Thread a resume through run_game -> drive_game_loop, and have the scheduler executor / retry seam rehydrate via US-249 before a re-attempt so a previously-started game resumes from its persisted tail instead of colliding. On the FIRST attempt of a fresh game resume is None (clean start); on a RE-attempt of a game that already has events past GameCreated, rehydrate and resume.
acceptanceCriteria: run_game accepts a resume: GameResume | None = None keyword and threads it into drive_game_loop(..., resume=resume); a test asserts run_game with a resume resumes from the persisted phase (no second GameCreated emitted) and the hash chain continues unbroken.

The scheduler executor / retry seam (scheduler.py _default_game_executor + _run_with_retry, ~85-91 / ~221-258) rehydrates a previously-started game via the US-249 helper before a re-attempt and passes the resulting GameResume into run_game; a test asserts a benchmark game that crashed after GameCreated and is re-driven resumes (no uq_game_event_sequence IntegrityError) and completes.
The first attempt of a never-started game still passes resume=None (clean GameCreated at sequence 0); a test asserts the fresh path is unchanged.

The _persist_terminated_event COMPLETED-skip (game_runner.py ~499-505) still short-circuits a finalize on an already-COMPLETED game (a resumed-then-finalized game does not double-apply ratings); a test asserts an already-COMPLETED game's re-drive applies ratings zero extra times.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Sites: run_game (game_runner.py ~1272, add resume kw), drive_game_loop already accepts resume (~1019), scheduler _default_game_executor (~85-91) + _run_with_retry (~221-258). Bug confirmed: benchmark re-drive restarts at sequence 0 -> uq_game_event_sequence collision (models.py ~261). Rehydration helper is US-249. This makes the existing game_max_attempts retry actually work for crashes.

userStories.title: Alembic 0052: per-game lease columns (leased_by, lease_expires_at, attempt_count, last_error, last_error_kind) + index
userStories.description: The Game table (db/models.py ~187-216) has NO lease columns - so a crash-safe game-grain claim is impossible and a reaped-then-resurrected worker could double-finalize. Add Alembic migration 0052 (down_revision 0051, the current head) adding all-nullable additive lease/diagnostic columns to games: leased_by (String), lease_expires_at (DateTime tz), attempt_count (Integer), last_error (String/Text), last_error_kind (String); plus an index on (status, lease_expires_at) for the claim/reaper scan. Use batch_alter_table for SQLite compatibility (the repo's established additive-column idiom - see 0051/0047/0015). Add the matching SQLAlchemy column + Index declarations to the Game model so models and migration agree. No data migration; idempotent up/down.
acceptanceCriteria: Alembic migration 0052 (revision '0052', down_revision '0051') adds nullable columns to games: leased_by, lease_expires_at, attempt_count, last_error, last_error_kind, and an index on (status, lease_expires_at); the upgrade uses batch_alter_table so it applies on SQLite.
The migration is linear after head 0051 (a single-head assertion passes) and the downgrade drops exactly the added columns + index.

The Game SQLAlchemy model (db/models.py) gains the matching nullable columns + the Index declaration, AND a dedicated per-migration test (mirroring tests/db/test_migration_0051_lobby_integrity_ack.py) applies 0052 to a fresh SQLite DB via command.upgrade(head), asserts the EXACT added columns (leased_by, lease_expires_at, attempt_count, last_error, last_error_kind) + the (status, lease_expires_at) index exist and are dropped by downgrade, AND asserts the Game model declares the same columns/Index. NOTE: there is NO automated alembic metadata-diff guard, and the default 'pytest -m not integration' gate builds the test DB from the MODELS (Base.metadata.create_all), NOT migrations - so this hand-written per-migration assertion is the ONLY model<->migration agreement check that runs by default. All columns default NULL so existing rows are unaffected.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Current head = 0051_lobby_integrity_acknowledged (down chain 0049->0050->0051). Game model at db/models.py ~187-216 has NO lease columns today. batch_alter_table idiom used in 0051/0047/0015. All-nullable additive so a re-run of an in-flight game is safe. Index (status, lease_expires_at) feeds US-252's claim + reaper.

userStories.title: Game-grain claim + continuous stale-game reaper (DB-as-queue, FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED)
userStories.description: With lease columns present (US-251), add a game-grain claim and a stale-game reaper modeled on the gauntlet repo's claim_oldest_pending (FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED on PostgreSQL only, no-op on single-writer SQLite) and reset_stale_running (db/repositories/gauntlets.py ~101-135 / ~183-206). claim_oldest_pending_game atomically picks an eligible game (CREATED or a RUNNING game whose lease has expired), stamps leased_by + lease_expires_at + bumps attempt_count, and returns it; reset_stale_games clears the lease on RUNNING games whose lease_expires_at is past so they become re-claimable. Run the reaper as a CONTINUOUS gated tick inside run_scheduler (not only at startup) so a crashed worker's in-flight games are reclaimed mid-run. The lease clock is INJECTED (the scheduler's Clock), never read in a repo via wall-clock.
acceptanceCriteria: db/repositories/games gains claim_oldest_pending_game(session, *, now, lease_ttl, worker_id) that selects an eligible game (status CREATED, or RUNNING with lease_expires_at <= now) with .with_for_update(skip_locked=True) on PostgreSQL only, sets leased_by=worker_id, lease_expires_at=now+ttl, increments attempt_count, and returns it (or None); a test asserts two concurrent claimers never claim the same game on PostgreSQL and the single-writer SQLite path still claims correctly.

db/repositories/games gains reset_stale_games(session, *, now) that clears leased_by/lease_expires_at on RUNNING games whose lease is expired and returns their ids; a test asserts an expired-lease game is reclaimable and a fresh-lease game is not.

run_scheduler runs reset_stale_games on a CONTINUOUS gated tick (gated by a settings flag/interval), not only at startup; a test asserts a stale in-flight game is reaped during the loop (not just before it starts).

The lease clock is injected (the scheduler Clock / now passed in); no repo reads datetime.utcnow()/time.time() - a test asserts the reaper uses the injected clock (advancing the injected clock changes which games are stale).
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Template: gauntlets repo claim_oldest_pending (~101-135, .with_for_update(skip_locked=True) PostgreSQL-only) + reset_stale_running (~183-206). Lease columns from US-251. The reaper must be a CONTINUOUS tick in run_scheduler (the gauntlet recovery runs at startup; this must run mid-loop). Single-host: ONE worker near-term, but the claim/reaper make a restart crash-safe. Clock injected per AGENTS.md hard rule 1.

userStories.title: Lease-fence re-check inside the finalize transaction (no double-finalize after resurrection)
userStories.description: A reaped-then-resurrected worker must not double-finalize a game whose lease was reassigned to another claimer. Add a lease-fence re-check inside the finalize transaction (the _persist_terminated_event / mark-complete path in game_runner.py): within the same transaction that writes the terminal event + applies ratings, re-read the game row and assert leased_by == this worker AND the lease is not expired; if the fence fails (the lease was stolen/reaped), abort the finalize WITHOUT writing the terminal event or ratings (a no-op, leaving the live holder to finalize). This composes with the existing COMPLETED-skip (~499-505) which already prevents re-finalizing an already-COMPLETED game.
acceptanceCriteria: The finalize transaction re-reads the game row and proceeds only if leased_by == the finalizing worker_id AND lease_expires_at is in the future (relative to the injected clock); if the fence fails it aborts the finalize (no terminal event, no rating writes) - a test asserts a worker whose lease was reassigned does NOT write the terminal event or any rating rows.

The fence check happens INSIDE the same transaction as the terminal-event write / rating application (not a separate read-then-write), so it is not itself TOCTOU - a test demonstrates the fence and the finalize are atomic (the fence read and the write share one transaction/session.begin).
The happy path is unchanged: a worker holding a valid lease finalizes normally and applies ratings exactly once; a test asserts the valid-lease finalize writes the terminal event + ratings.
Composes with the COMPLETED-skip (game_runner.py ~499-505): an already-COMPLETED game still short-circuits; a test asserts no double rating application across both guards.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Site: game_runner.py _persist_terminated_event (~481-584; COMPLETED-skip ~499-505). worker_id is currently used ONLY for the scheduler heartbeat/tick (scheduler.py ~517, 527-531) and is NOT yet threaded to the finalize site - US-253 must add the plumbing: run_scheduler -> _drive_gauntlet -> _run_child_games -> _run_one_game -> GamePersistence (new worker_id field) -> _persist_terminated_event. The fence must be inside the finalize transaction to be non-TOCTOU. Lease columns from US-251, claim from US-252. Single-worker near-term, but this makes resurrection safe.

userStories.title: Retryable-vs-poison exception classification at the retry seam (transient -> retry+backoff; replay/hash/validation -> dead-letter)
userStories.description: The scheduler retry seam (scheduler.py _run_with_retry ~221-258) retries on a bare except Exception up to game_max_attempts, then marks the game FAILED. This blindly retries poison errors (a replay-hash mismatch, a validation/contract error) that will deterministically fail again, wasting budget and time, while a transient provider/transport error deserves retry+backoff. Add an exception classification at the retry seam: provider/transport/timeout errors are RETRYABLE (retry with backoff up to max_attempts); replay/hash/validation/contract errors are POISON (dead-letter immediately, recording last_error + last_error_kind, no further attempts). This makes the retry budget meaningful and feeds the campaign dead-letter path (US-260).
acceptanceCriteria: A classification helper maps exceptions to RETRYABLE vs POISON: provider/transport/timeout (e.g. the LLM adapter's provider-error / timeout types) -> RETRYABLE; replay/hash mismatch (ReplayHashMismatchError), validation/contract errors -> POISON; a unit test asserts each category classifies correctly.

The retry seam retries only RETRYABLE errors (up to game_max_attempts, with a backoff hook), and on a POISON error stops immediately (no further attempts) and marks the game FAILED with last_error/last_error_kind stamped (the lease columns from US-251); a test asserts a poison error fails after exactly one attempt and a retryable error retries up to max_attempts.
last_error and last_error_kind are persisted on the game row for a failed/dead-lettered game; a test asserts the failure kind is recoverable from the row (for the campaign report's documented holes).
Backoff is deterministic/injected for tests (no real sleep in the unit test - the sleeper/backoff is injectable); a test asserts the backoff hook is invoked between retryable attempts.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Site: scheduler.py _run_with_retry (~221-258, bare except Exception today). game_max_attempts default 2 (DEFAULT_GAME_MAX_ATTEMPTS ~62). last_error/last_error_kind columns from US-251. ReplayHashMismatchError comes from replay_event_log. Sleeper is already injectable in run_scheduler (Sleeper type). Feeds campaign dead-letter (US-260).

userStories.title: Fix wall-clock gauntlet seed (seed-only derivation for cross-run reproducibility)
userStories.description: The scheduled_gauntlet job derives its gauntlet seed with WALL-CLOCK time: gauntlet_seed=f"scheduled:{schedule_id}:{now.isoformat()}" (scheduler/gauntlet_job.py ~80). This breaks cross-run reproducibility - the same schedule firing at different wall-clock instants produces different seeds, different boards, different genealogy. Campaigns must replay byte-stable, so the seed must derive from stable inputs only (schedule_id + a deterministic counter/run index), matching the seed-only idiom used by matchmaker._derive_gauntlet_seed (sha256(b"matchmaker:" + seed.encode() + b":" + str(history_len).encode())) and gauntlets/scheduler.derive_game_seed (sha256(b"game" + gauntlet_seed.encode() + index.to_bytes(4))). Replace the wall-clock component with a deterministic run-index / occurrence counter so re-running a schedule reproduces the same seed.
acceptanceCriteria: The scheduled gauntlet seed no longer embeds the injected now (or any value derived from now); it derives via the established sha256(b'scheduled:' + str(schedule_id).encode() + b':' + ...) idiom from STABLE inputs only - schedule_id plus the cron-computed SCHEDULED FIRE INSTANT for this occurrence (row.next_run_at as read at fire time, BEFORE mark_run advances it - deterministic from schedule_cron, NOT the injected now); a test asserts two runs of the same schedule at different injected wall-clock `now` values produce the SAME gauntlet_seed.

Distinct occurrences get distinct seeds (the scheduled fire instant advances per occurrence); a test runs the job, mark_run (advancing next_run_at), runs again, and asserts occurrence N and N+1 produce DIFFERENT seeds. No new column/migration is added.
Existing scheduled-gauntlet behavior (which league/ruleset/clone_count it creates) is otherwise unchanged; a test asserts the only change is the seed source.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Site: scheduler/gauntlet_job.py ~80 (f'scheduled:{schedule_id}:{now.isoformat()}'). Reference idioms: matchmaker._derive_gauntlet_seed (~43-47), gauntlets/scheduler.derive_game_seed (~46-48), derive_pair_id (~51-56). PINNED SOURCE: use row.next_run_at (the cron-computed scheduled fire instant for the occurrence being fired, read BEFORE mark_run recomputes it via next_run_at(schedule_cron, after=now)) as the per-occurrence stable input - deterministic from schedule_cron, NOT the injected now. Do NOT use now/last_run_at (fails the cross-run-same-seed AC#1) and do NOT add a run-count column (ScheduledGauntlet has none; Gauntlet has no schedule_id FK). Reproducibility is load-bearing for campaign replay (P3).

userStories.title: CI replay test: interrupted+resumed benchmark game == uninterrupted (byte-identical final event_hash)
userStories.description: Prove the resilience backbone preserves determinism: a benchmark game interrupted mid-phase and resumed (via US-249/US-250) must yield a byte-identical final event_hash and event-hash-chain to the same game run uninterrupted. This is the CI guarantee that resume does not perturb the deterministic engine (no duplicate events, no re-emitted GameCreated, no reseeded RNG). Use a DeterministicMockAdapter so the run is fully deterministic, drive a game to a mid-game phase, persist, simulate a crash, rehydrate, resume to terminal, and compare the final event_hash + chain digest against an uninterrupted reference run of the same game_seed.
acceptanceCriteria: A test runs a benchmark game to completion uninterrupted (DeterministicMockAdapter, fixed game_seed) and records its terminal_result + final event_hash + event-hash-chain digest.

A second test run of the SAME game_seed is interrupted mid-phase (state persisted to events), rehydrated via the US-249 helper, and resumed via US-250 to terminal; the test asserts its terminal_result + final event_hash + chain digest are BYTE-IDENTICAL to the uninterrupted run.
The test asserts no duplicate GameCreated and no sequence collision occur on resume (the resumed tail continues the chain from the persisted sequence, not from 0).
The test runs under the default pytest -m 'not integration' gate (mock adapter, no real provider; no pytest.mark.integration).
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Adapter: llm/mock.DeterministicMockAdapter (deterministic per (phase_id, public_player_id)). Hash rule: event_hash = sha256(prev + canonical_json(event_without_hash_or_timestamp)) (AGENTS.md hard rule 4). This is the byte-identical-resume CI proof from the plan. Depends on US-250 (resume wiring) and US-253 (fence so finalize is once-only).

userStories.title: Pure-core deterministic pairing-matrix generator (coverage-guaranteed subset, balanced faction/role exposure)
userStories.description: Add a NEW pure-core module core/scheduling/pairing.py (the core/scheduling/ package exists today with only cron helpers; it is pure). Given (campaign_seed: str, ruleset_id: str, model_field: list[model_id], format in {MIRROR, ROUND_ROBIN}, per_model_game_target), produce an ORDERED list of (cell_index, roster) as a COVERAGE-GUARANTEED SUBSET for a LARGE field (20+ models) - NOT exhaustive round-robin. player_count is DERIVED from core.rulesets.get_ruleset(ruleset_id).PLAYER_COUNT (NOT passed as a bare int) - the balance guarantee needs the Ruleset's faction/role layout, which a bare player_count cannot provide. Each model must meet enough DISTINCT opponents and get BALANCED (guaranteed, not in-expectation) TOWN/MAFIA and role-family exposure to hit the per-model game target. MIRROR is the canonical comparison unit: the canonical context schedules ONLY mirror pairs (each cell yields a mirror pair sharing one game_seed, legs differing only by seat order - reuse the derive_pair_id / pair_leg concept from gauntlets/scheduler). The per-cell seed derives from sha256(b'campaign:' + campaign_seed.encode() + cell_index.to_bytes(...)) so the whole matrix replays byte-stable. Use SeededRng for all selection/shuffle - NO random/secrets/datetime/wall-clock (hard rule 1). Faction/role-family balance is computed INSIDE the pure module from the per-cell role layout via core.rulesets.get_ruleset(ruleset_id) + core.engine.role_assignment.assign_roles(per_cell_game_seed, ruleset) (both pure; assign_roles REQUIRES a Ruleset object) - NOT gauntlets/evaluation.ModelSeatCounts, which imports sqlalchemy + db.models and is impure - so 'balanced exposure' is a guaranteed structural property of the emitted matrix, not a statistical hope. SWISS/rating-adaptive pairing is OUT OF SCOPE (encode as a docstring note).
acceptanceCriteria: core/scheduling/pairing.py exposes a pure function (e.g. generate_pairing_matrix(campaign_seed, ruleset_id, model_field, *, format, per_model_game_target)) returning an ordered list of (cell_index, roster) where roster is a tuple of model ids of length get_ruleset(ruleset_id).PLAYER_COUNT; MIRROR format yields cells whose canonical comparison is a mirror pair (shared per-cell game_seed, two seat-order legs); the module imports SeededRng (and core.rulesets.get_ruleset / core.engine.role_assignment.assign_roles, both pure) and uses NO random/secrets/datetime/time.

COVERAGE GUARANTEE: for a large field (>=20 models, player_count 7 or 10), every model appears in at least enough cells to hit per_model_game_target and meets at least a configured number of DISTINCT opponents; a property test (hypothesis or parametrized) asserts the coverage target is met for several field sizes - it is a SUBSET (cell count << exhaustive round-robin choose-k).

BALANCED EXPOSURE GUARANTEE (not in-expectation): per model, TOWN vs MAFIA seat counts across the emitted matrix are within a stated tolerance, and role-family exposure is balanced within tolerance; a property test asserts the per-model faction balance holds for the generated matrix, computed via get_ruleset(ruleset_id) + assign_roles(per_cell_game_seed, ruleset) INSIDE the pure module (NOT the impure gauntlets/evaluation.ModelSeatCounts).

BYTE-STABLE REPLAY: the per-cell seed derives from sha256(b'campaign:' + campaign_seed + cell_index); a test asserts generate_pairing_matrix is byte-identical across two calls with the same inputs and stable across process runs (no set/dict-ordering nondeterminism).
A purity-firewall test asserts core/scheduling/pairing.py imports none of random/secrets/datetime/time and no DB/provider modules (extend or mirror the existing core purity test).
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Location: core/scheduling/ (exists, pure - currently only cron helpers). Building blocks: core/engine/rng.SeededRng (next_bytes/randbelow/shuffle/choice), core/seating.seat_permutation, core.rulesets.get_ruleset (pure, by id), core/engine/role_assignment.assign_roles(game_seed, ruleset) (deterministic; REQUIRES a Ruleset, hence ruleset_id in the signature), gauntlets/scheduler derive_pair_id/derive_game_seed/pair_leg (mirror pair concept). Seed prefix b'campaign:' per the plan. Do NOT import gauntlets/evaluation.ModelSeatCounts (impure). SWISS/rating-adaptive DEFERRED (out of scope, note in docstring). This module is INDEPENDENT (no P1/P3 dep) - pure core.

userStories.title: Alembic 0053: campaigns + campaign_pairings tables + nullable gauntlets.campaign_id + campaign owner/lease columns
userStories.description: Persist the Campaign entity and its pairing-matrix ledger. Add Alembic migration 0053 (down_revision 0052) creating: a campaigns table (id, campaign_seed, ruleset_id, league_id, format, player_count, per_model_game_target, status PENDING/RUNNING/COMPLETED, owner/lease columns leased_by + lease_expires_at + heartbeat_at, created_at, completed_at, plus convergence-target columns sigma_target + rank_stability_k), a campaign_pairings ledger table (id, campaign_id FK, cell_index, roster_json, status PENDING/MATERIALIZED/COMPLETED/DEAD_LETTER, attempt_count, last_error, gauntlet_id nullable FK once materialized, with a unique constraint on (campaign_id, cell_index)), and a nullable gauntlets.campaign_id FK + index. Add the matching SQLAlchemy models. batch_alter_table for the gauntlets.campaign_id add (SQLite). The ledger persists the WHOLE matrix's cells up front but materializes gauntlets/games INCREMENTALLY (US-259) - the rows here are cheap ledger rows, not thousands of game rows.
acceptanceCriteria: Alembic migration 0053 (revision '0053', down_revision '0052') creates campaigns (with owner/lease columns leased_by/lease_expires_at/heartbeat_at and convergence-target columns) and campaign_pairings (cell ledger with status, attempt_count, last_error, nullable gauntlet_id FK, unique (campaign_id, cell_index)), and adds a nullable gauntlets.campaign_id FK + index via batch_alter_table; matching SQLAlchemy models are added. Within upgrade(), create_table('campaigns') + create_table('campaign_pairings') run BEFORE the batch_alter_table that adds gauntlets.campaign_id (SQLite runs migrations with PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON via db/base.py's connect listener, so the batch rebuild's INSERT...SELECT row-copy must be able to resolve the campaigns FK); within downgrade(), the gauntlets.campaign_id column/FK is dropped BEFORE drop_table('campaigns'). A dedicated per-migration SQLite test (mirroring the 0047/0051 pattern) applies 0053 and asserts the campaigns/campaign_pairings columns + the unique (campaign_id, cell_index) constraint + the gauntlets.campaign_id column/index exist and are dropped exactly by downgrade, AND that the matching models declare them; the new table names are added to EXPECTED_TABLES in BOTH tests/db/test_migrations.py and tests/db/test_migrations_all_dialects.py. NOTE: the default gate builds the DB from models, not migrations, so this per-migration test is the only default-gate migration check (the generic EXPECTED_TABLES intersection check cannot catch a leaked new table on its own).
The migration is linear after 0052 (single-head assertion passes) and the downgrade drops the two tables + the gauntlets.campaign_id column/index exactly.
campaign_pairings has a unique constraint on (campaign_id, cell_index) so a cell cannot be double-materialized; a test asserts inserting a duplicate (campaign_id, cell_index) raises IntegrityError.
gauntlets.campaign_id is nullable so existing/standalone gauntlets (not part of a campaign) are unaffected; a test asserts a campaign-less gauntlet still creates.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Chain: 0052 (US-251) -> 0053 here. Gauntlet model at db/models.py ~142-159 (no campaign_id today). campaign_pairings is a cheap LEDGER (one row per pairing cell); games/gauntlets materialize incrementally (US-259). status DEAD_LETTER for exhausted cells (US-260). Convergence-target columns (sigma_target, rank_stability_k) feed US-269 publish gate. owner/lease columns mirror the gauntlet heartbeat pattern for crash-safe campaign claim (US-259).

userStories.title: Campaign repo + create-from-matrix + claim/lease/heartbeat + incremental materialization from the ledger
userStories.description: Add a campaign_repo and the create + claim + INCREMENTAL materialization logic. create_campaign_from_matrix(campaign_seed, ruleset_id, model_field, format, ...) calls the US-257 generator (which emits MODEL-ID rosters), RESOLVES each model_id to a canonical active agent_build_id (e.g. via agent_builds.list_(active=True) / model_config lookup, choosing one canonical build per model) and stores the resolved agent_build_id UUID roster in campaign_pairings.roster_json (create_paired_gauntlet's roster is list[agent_build_id UUID], NOT model ids), persists the campaign row + all campaign_pairings cells (PENDING) - but materializes NO gauntlets/games up front. The campaign row carries league_id + ruleset_id; the prompt_version_id needed by create_paired_gauntlet is sourced from the resolved AgentBuilds (or stored on the campaign row). claim_campaign / heartbeat / reset_stale mirror the gauntlet claim_oldest_pending + update_heartbeat + reset_stale_running pattern (FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED PostgreSQL-only; injected clock). A materialization step picks the next batch of PENDING cells (BOUNDED batch size, settings-driven), creates the child gauntlet/games for each via the existing gauntlets/scheduler.create_paired_gauntlet(session, *, league_id, ruleset_id, prompt_version_id, pair_count, gauntlet_seed, roster) - passing the campaign's league_id/ruleset_id, the sourced prompt_version_id, the cell's resolved agent_build_id roster, and the cell's per-cell seed sha256(b'campaign:'+campaign_seed+cell_index) as the gauntlet_seed argument (create_paired_gauntlet derives per-pair game_seeds from it internally) - flips the cells to MATERIALIZED, and links gauntlet_id back to the cell. RESUME reads the LEDGER (cell statuses), not in-flight rows - so a restart re-derives what to materialize from persisted cell state, never losing or double-creating cells (the unique (campaign_id, cell_index) constraint is the fence).
acceptanceCriteria: campaign_repo.create_campaign_from_matrix resolves the generator's model-id rosters to canonical active agent_build_id UUIDs, persists a campaign row + one campaign_pairings cell per generated cell (status PENDING, roster_json holding the resolved agent_build_id UUIDs) and creates ZERO gauntlets/games at creation time; a test asserts cell count == matrix length, roster_json holds agent_build_id UUIDs, and gauntlet/game count == 0 right after creation.

campaign_repo gains claim_campaign (FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED on PostgreSQL, injected now, sets leased_by/lease_expires_at), update_heartbeat, and reset_stale_campaigns mirroring the gauntlet repo; a test asserts a stale-lease campaign is reclaimable and a fresh-lease one is not.

A materialize_next_batch step selects up to a bounded (settings-driven) number of PENDING cells, creates each cell's child gauntlet/games via create_paired_gauntlet (passing league_id, ruleset_id, the sourced prompt_version_id, the cell's resolved agent_build_id roster, and the per-cell seed sha256(b'campaign:'+campaign_seed+cell_index) as the gauntlet_seed argument), flips the cells to MATERIALIZED, and stamps gauntlet_id on the cell; a test asserts only the bounded batch is materialized per call, the roster passed is agent_build_id UUIDs (not model-id strings), and the cell->gauntlet link is set.

RESUME-from-ledger: a test materializes one batch, simulates a worker restart (fresh session), and asserts the next materialize call resumes from the persisted cell statuses (no PENDING cell is skipped and no MATERIALIZED cell is re-created - the unique (campaign_id, cell_index) constraint prevents double-materialization).
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Repos to mirror: gauntlets repo create/claim_oldest_pending/update_heartbeat/reset_stale_running (~24-206). create_paired_gauntlet from gauntlets/scheduler (signature: league_id, ruleset_id, prompt_version_id, pair_count, gauntlet_seed: str, roster: list[agent_build_id UUID]; mirror pair, pair_leg 0/1, derives per-pair game_seeds from gauntlet_seed). KEY GAP to close: the US-257 generator emits MODEL IDS, but create_paired_gauntlet needs agent_build_id UUIDs AND a prompt_version_id - resolve model_id -> canonical active agent_build at create_campaign_from_matrix time, store the resolved UUID roster on the cell's roster_json, and source prompt_version_id from the chosen AgentBuilds (a 'model id' is NOT an agent_build_id in this codebase: ModelConfig vs AgentBuild are distinct, one model -> potentially many builds). Generator is US-257. INCREMENTAL: the ledger holds all cells, but gauntlets/games are created in bounded batches - never thousands up front (locked decision). Resume reads the ledger, not in-flight rows. Clock injected. game-grain claim from US-252 drains the materialized games.

userStories.title: Dead-letter a pairing cell after bounded retries (campaign documents the hole, does not block)
userStories.description: A pairing cell whose child games keep failing must not stall the campaign forever. When a cell's games exhaust their bounded retries (a POISON error from US-254, or attempt_count exceeds a settings-driven cell-max-attempts), mark the campaign_pairings cell DEAD_LETTER (recording last_error) instead of leaving it PENDING/MATERIALIZED. A DEAD_LETTER cell is terminal-for-scheduling (never re-materialized, never re-claimed) and the campaign treats it as a DOCUMENTED hole rather than a blocker - the campaign can still auto-finalize (US-261) with DEAD_LETTER cells, and the report (US-268) documents them. This is the per-cell analogue of FAILED/poison handling.
acceptanceCriteria: A cell whose games exhaust bounded retries (POISON classification from US-254, or attempt_count >= settings cell-max-attempts) is flipped to DEAD_LETTER with last_error recorded; a test asserts a permanently-failing cell ends DEAD_LETTER after exactly the bounded attempts, not earlier and not looping forever.
A DEAD_LETTER cell is never re-materialized or re-claimed (it is terminal for scheduling); a test asserts a DEAD_LETTER cell is excluded from materialize_next_batch and from any cell re-selection.
A DEAD_LETTER cell does NOT block campaign progress: the remaining PENDING/MATERIALIZED cells continue; a test asserts a campaign with one DEAD_LETTER cell still drives its other cells to completion.
The DEAD_LETTER cell's failure (last_error / kind) is recoverable from the row for the campaign report's documented-holes section (US-268); a test asserts the failure detail is queryable.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Cell status DEAD_LETTER from the US-258 schema. POISON classification from US-254 (replay/hash/validation -> immediate dead-letter; transient -> retry). cell-max-attempts is settings-driven. The campaign AUTO-FINALIZES with DEAD_LETTER cells (locked decision: no hard operator block); the report documents the hole (US-268).

userStories.title: Tighten gauntlet/campaign completion: terminal-only + faction/role balance gate (fix loose _drive_gauntlet)
userStories.description: Gauntlet completion is loose: _drive_gauntlet (runner/scheduler.py ~309-392) collects child-game failures, LOGS them, but unconditionally calls gauntlets_repo.mark_completed regardless of FAILED children (~382-384); and gauntlets/completion._all_games_terminal checks only status==COMPLETED, ignoring FAILED. TIGHTEN completion so a gauntlet (standalone OR campaign) is COMPLETED only when EVERY child GAME is TERMINAL - COMPLETED or FAILED (retries are synchronous in _run_with_retry, so there is no persisted 'mid-retry' game status; only CREATED/RUNNING are non-terminal and block completion) - AND faction/role balance over the gauntlet's COMPLETED games (those with GameSeat rows) is within tolerance (gate on ModelSeatCounts from gauntlets/evaluation; a gauntlet with no completed games / no seats, or any FAILED-exhausted leg, is exempt and still finalizes). A campaign finalizes only when EVERY cell is terminal (COMPLETED or DEAD_LETTER). Expose an 'X of N cells done' progress count. The campaign AUTO-FINALIZES (no hard operator block) once every cell is terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: _drive_gauntlet/finalize no longer marks a gauntlet COMPLETED while a child game is non-terminal (status CREATED = not yet driven, or RUNNING = crashed mid-drive - there is NO persisted 'retrying' status; retries are synchronous in _run_with_retry so a returned child is always COMPLETED or FAILED); a gauntlet is COMPLETED only when EVERY child game is terminal (COMPLETED, or FAILED = retry-exhausted/poison) AND the per-model faction balance gate (see below) is satisfied. This applies to ALL gauntlets - STANDALONE and campaign alike; FAILED is terminal for any gauntlet (a campaign cell additionally maps a FAILED-exhausted gauntlet to DEAD_LETTER). A test asserts a gauntlet with a CREATED/RUNNING child is NOT marked COMPLETED, and one with all-terminal+balanced children IS.

gauntlets/completion._all_games_terminal (or its caller) treats COMPLETED and FAILED children as terminal-for-completion (a permanently-failed child does not block completion forever) while a CREATED or RUNNING child blocks; a test asserts both directions.

A campaign is COMPLETED only when EVERY campaign_pairings cell is terminal (COMPLETED or DEAD_LETTER); the campaign AUTO-FINALIZES at that point (no operator block); a test asserts a campaign with one DEAD_LETTER + rest COMPLETED auto-finalizes, and one with a PENDING cell does not.
Progress is exposed as 'X of N cells done' (and X of N gauntlet children) queryable from the campaign/gauntlet state; a test asserts the count reflects terminal cells.

The faction/role balance gate uses ModelSeatCounts (gauntlets/evaluation) within a settings-driven tolerance, computed ONLY over the gauntlet's COMPLETED games that have GameSeat rows; a gauntlet with ZERO completed games (all children FAILED-exhausted) or no GameSeat rows is balance-trivially-satisfied and still finalizes; a gauntlet/cell containing any FAILED-exhausted leg is EXEMPT from the balance gate (the surviving-game imbalance is a documented hole for US-268, not a completion blocker). A test asserts a fully-COMPLETED imbalanced gauntlet is NOT marked COMPLETED until balance is within tolerance, and a gauntlet with a FAILED leg still finalizes.

NO REGRESSION of existing scheduler tests: a STANDALONE gauntlet (campaign_id IS NULL) with a permanently-FAILED child still finalizes COMPLETED. The existing default-gate tests in tests/runner/test_scheduler.py - test_child_game_failure_isolated_and_logged, test_child_game_exhausts_bounded_attempts_and_writes_no_ratings, test_pending_gauntlet_runs_and_finalizes_to_completed, test_crash_recovery_resets_stale_running_gauntlet, test_stop_event_finishes_in_flight_gauntlet_before_returning, test_concurrency_cap_honored_via_injected_semaphore - MUST continue to pass UNCHANGED (their _FakeExecutor flips game status WITHOUT writing GameSeat rows, so the balance gate MUST NOT require seats those tests never create, and the gauntlets they drive MUST reach COMPLETED rather than burning the watcher's poll budget).
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Bug confirmed: _drive_gauntlet (~309-392) marks COMPLETED despite FAILED children (mark_completed at ~382-384, no failure gate); completion._all_games_terminal checks status==COMPLETED only (~100). ModelSeatCounts (gauntlets/evaluation ~101-109: town_seats/mafia_seats/total_seats). DEAD_LETTER cells (US-260) are terminal-for-completion. Campaign auto-finalize is the locked decision (no hard block).

userStories.title: Campaign TickHook wired into run_scheduler (composes with the existing scheduler loop)
userStories.description: Drive the campaign through the existing scheduler seam rather than a new loop. run_scheduler already calls a tick_hook(now) once per tick (runner/scheduler.py ~533-534) and scheduler/bootstrap.build_scheduled_gauntlet_tick_hook composes several behaviors into that hook. Add a campaign tick (build_campaign_tick_hook or fold into the bootstrap composer) that, per tick: claims a campaign (or heartbeats the held one), materializes the next bounded batch of cells (US-259), drives/finalizes per US-261, and reaps stale campaigns (reset_stale_campaigns). It must COMPOSE with the existing tick hook (both run), not replace it. The injected clock is the scheduler's tick clock - no wall-clock in the hook body beyond the injected now.
acceptanceCriteria: A campaign tick hook is added and composed into run_scheduler's tick_hook (alongside the existing scheduled-gauntlet/matchmaking/retention hooks) so both run each tick; a test asserts the campaign tick advances a campaign (materializes a batch / finalizes) when invoked via the composed hook, AND the pre-existing tick behaviors still fire.

Per tick the campaign hook claims-or-heartbeats a campaign, materializes the next bounded batch, and reaps stale campaigns using the injected now; a test asserts a multi-tick run materializes the campaign in bounded batches across ticks (not all at once) and finalizes when all cells terminal.
The hook is gated by a settings flag (like padrino_enable_continuous_matchmaking / padrino_enable_retention) so it can be turned off; a test asserts the gate disables the campaign tick.
The hook uses the injected scheduler clock (no datetime.utcnow()/time.time() in the hook); a test asserts advancing the injected clock drives heartbeat/stale logic.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Seam: run_scheduler tick_hook (~533-534), TickHook = Callable[[datetime], Awaitable[None]] (~82). Composer: scheduler/bootstrap.build_scheduled_gauntlet_tick_hook (~28-79; composes matchmaking/retention behind settings flags). Single worker near-term: the campaign claim/heartbeat make a restart resume the same campaign. Game-grain drain is US-252; materialization US-259; completion US-261.

userStories.title: Shared atomic budget-reservation module + Alembic 0054 reservation slot table
userStories.description: spend_governor.can_start_game is SELECT-sum-then-compare (cumulative_spend_usd then 'if spent >= cap: return False', economics/spend_governor.py ~25-48) - a TOCTOU race: between the SELECT and the game start, concurrent admissions can overshoot the cap. Generalize the proven atomic finite-slot reservation from economics/human_cost_governance._claim_inference_slot (~417-461): divide a budget into floor(budget/reserve) finite slots; already-charged spend implicitly consumes the lowest slots; an explicit reservation row (unique on scope) claims the next free index; concurrent claimers race the unique constraint inside session.begin_nested() and retry; fail-closed (return None) when exhausted - so concurrent admissions can NEVER overshoot the ceiling. Add Alembic migration 0054 (down_revision 0053) for a generic budget reservation slot table (modeled on HumanInferenceReservation: scope_key, slot_index, reserved_at, optional binding + released_at, unique on (scope_key, slot_index[, partition])), and a shared reservation module exposing claim_budget_slot / release_budget_slot usable by both global and per-campaign scopes. This module is the enforcement primitive; US-264 wires it into admission.
acceptanceCriteria: Alembic migration 0054 (revision '0054', down_revision '0053') creates a generic budget reservation slot table modeled on HumanInferenceReservation (scope_key, slot_index, reserved_at, nullable binding, nullable released_at, unique constraint covering (scope_key, slot_index) plus any partition key); matching SQLAlchemy model added; a dedicated per-migration SQLite test (0047/0051 pattern) applies 0054 and asserts the slot-table columns + unique constraint exist and are dropped by downgrade AND the model declares them; the new table name is added to EXPECTED_TABLES in BOTH tests/db/test_migrations.py and tests/db/test_migrations_all_dialects.py; single-head + downgrade-drops-exactly assertions pass.

A shared reservation module exposes claim_budget_slot(session, *, scope_key, spent_usd, budget_usd, reserve_usd, now) -> slot_id | None and release_budget_slot, generalizing _claim_inference_slot: finite slots = floor(budget/reserve), implicit-used from spent, next-free-index claim inside session.begin_nested(), IntegrityError -> retry, exhausted -> None (fail-closed); a unit test asserts the cap is never overshot under concurrent claims (N concurrent claimers against a cap of K slots yield at most K successes).
A test asserts fail-closed: when budget is exhausted (or budget<=reserve) claim returns None and no slot row is created; and release frees a slot for reuse.
The module is scope-agnostic (a global scope_key and a per-campaign scope_key both work through the same primitive); a test asserts two different scope_keys maintain independent slot counts.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Template: economics/human_cost_governance._claim_inference_slot (~417-461: floor(budget/reserve) slots, implicit-used rounding, begin_nested + unique constraint + IntegrityError retry, fail-closed None). HumanInferenceReservation columns (models.py ~379-418: scope_key, reservation_day, slot_index, reserved_at, lobby_id/member_id, released_at; unique uq_human_inference_reservation_slot). TOCTOU confirmed in spend_governor (~25-48). This is the primitive; US-264 wires the BENCHMARK admission path. Independent of P1/P3.

userStories.title: Hard global + per-campaign budget ceiling on the benchmark admission path (game-grain, never kills an active game)
userStories.description: The main scheduler drain path enforces NO budget cap - economics/admission.admit() only gates continuous_matchmaking + judge_sampling, not the gauntlet/campaign drain. Wire the atomic reservation (US-263) into the BENCHMARK admission path at GAME GRAIN: before starting a game (in the scheduler executor / campaign drive), atomically reserve a budget slot against BOTH a GLOBAL scope (padrino_global_spend_cap_usd) AND the PER-CAMPAIGN scope (a per-campaign cap); if either reservation fails (cap reached), HALT admission of NEW games for that scope - but NEVER kill an in-flight game (admit at game grain, let running games finish). The halt is a soft stop: the campaign pauses claiming new cells and resumes when budget frees / cap is raised. Per-model game target ~50 GLOBAL + ~50/faction informs the per-campaign sizing but there are NO per-model $ sub-budgets (out of scope).
acceptanceCriteria: Before a benchmark game starts (scheduler executor / campaign materialize-and-claim path), an atomic budget slot is reserved against the GLOBAL cap AND the per-campaign cap via the US-263 primitive; if either is exhausted the game is NOT started and admission halts for that scope; a test asserts a campaign at its per-campaign cap stops starting NEW games while a different campaign under cap continues.

Hard ceiling, no overshoot: a test asserts that under concurrent game-starts against a global cap, the number of admitted games is bounded by the cap (the atomic reservation prevents the TOCTOU overshoot that spend_governor's SELECT-sum allowed).

NEVER kills an active game: a test asserts that when the cap is reached mid-campaign, already-running games run to completion (the halt only refuses NEW admissions); intra-game hard cost kill is explicitly OUT of scope.
The halt is recoverable: a test asserts that releasing budget (or raising the cap setting) lets the campaign resume admitting games on the next tick.

The benchmark admission path no longer relies on the TOCTOU SELECT-sum for the hard ceiling; spend_governor.can_start_game may remain as an advisory pre-check, but the AUTHORITATIVE gate is the atomic reservation; a test asserts the atomic gate is the one that bounds admission.

PER-CAMPAIGN SPEND ATTRIBUTION: the per-campaign reservation uses scope_key='campaign:'+campaign_id, and the per-campaign spent_usd passed to claim_budget_slot (US-263) is SUM(LlmCall.cost_usd) over games whose gauntlet belongs to this campaign (join LlmCall -> Game.gauntlet_id -> Gauntlet.campaign_id; gauntlets.campaign_id exists via US-258, reached transitively through US-262 -> US-259 -> US-258; campaign-less games are excluded). The GLOBAL scope keeps using cumulative_spend_usd (all LlmCall). A test asserts per-campaign spent_usd reflects only that campaign's games, so a campaign at its cap halts while a different campaign under its cap keeps admitting.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Sites: economics/admission.admit (gates only matchmaking+judge_sampling today), economics/spend_governor.can_start_game (TOCTOU ~25-48), the scheduler executor / campaign drive (US-262). Per-campaign spend join: LlmCall -> Game.gauntlet_id -> Gauntlet.campaign_id (campaign_id from US-258, available transitively via US-262 -> US-259 -> US-258; no campaign_id on Game/LlmCall directly). Settings: padrino_global_spend_cap_usd (~254, default 200.0) for global; add a per-campaign cap setting. Game-grain halt; never kills active games; no intra-game kill; no per-model $ sub-budgets (out of scope). Reservation primitive = US-263. Per-campaign scope ties to US-262's campaign drive.

userStories.title: Fix admission.py: treat FAILED as non-active (status != COMPLETED bug)
userStories.description: economics/admission.py counts concurrent/active games with the predicate Game.status != 'COMPLETED' (~line 83), which treats a FAILED game as ACTIVE - so a failed game wrongly consumes a concurrency slot until something flips it COMPLETED (which never happens for a terminally-FAILED game). Now that FAILED is a real terminal status (db/game_status.GAME_STATUS_FAILED, GAME_TERMINAL_STATUSES = {COMPLETED, FAILED}), fix admission to treat BOTH terminal statuses as non-active. Use the shared GAME_TERMINAL_STATUSES set rather than a literal so a future terminal status is handled automatically. This is a small correctness fix that prevents phantom concurrency consumption.
acceptanceCriteria: The active/concurrent-game predicate in economics/admission.py excludes BOTH COMPLETED and FAILED (use db/game_status.GAME_TERMINAL_STATUSES, not a status != 'COMPLETED' literal); a test asserts a FAILED game is NOT counted as active and does not consume a concurrency slot.
A test asserts the concurrency cap admits a new game when the only other games are terminal (COMPLETED or FAILED), and refuses when a genuinely RUNNING game is at the cap.
No other admission behavior changes (spend/daily gates unchanged); a test asserts the daily/spend gates still behave as before.
Audit for any other status != 'COMPLETED' active-predicate that should be terminal-aware (grep) and note/fix in the same story if it shares the bug; a test covers the corrected predicate.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Bug confirmed: admission.py ~83 select(func.count(Game.id)).where(Game.status != 'COMPLETED') ignores FAILED. Fix: use GAME_TERMINAL_STATUSES (db/game_status.py: {COMPLETED, FAILED}). Small independent correctness fix; complements US-264's hard cap. Grep for sibling status!='COMPLETED' predicates.

userStories.title: Immutable per-call cost: stamp price-table version + priced-at on LlmCall
userStories.description: Derived per-call cost (LiteLLM response_cost, with the token-price fallback price_turn_usd) is the trust basis for campaign spend, but it is currently MUTABLE in effect: LlmCall has cost_usd but NO record of WHICH price table produced it (no price-table-version / priced-at column - confirmed: LlmCall has cost_usd, tokens, model but no price_version). If the fallback rates (padrino_human_fallback_token_price_per_1k or any price table) change later, historical spend would be re-interpreted/rewritten. Make cost immutable by STAMPING the price-table version (or a content hash of the price table) and the priced-at-call-time onto LlmCall at the persist boundary, so historical spend is reproducible from the row alone and never changes when rates change. Additive nullable columns; no data migration. This is a NEW migration 0055 (revision '0055', down_revision '0054'): NEVER edit the already-shipped 0054 (or any prior migration 0001..0054) to append these columns - that desyncs any DB already at head and is the shipped-migration-edit hard-rule violation prior waves were burned by. US-266 depends on US-263 (0054) so the chain 0054 -> 0055 is DAG-enforced.
acceptanceCriteria: LlmCall gains nullable columns recording the pricing basis and the priced-at instant: a price_basis marker ('PROVIDER_RESPONSE_COST' when LiteLLM response_cost produced cost_usd, 'FALLBACK_TABLE' when the token-price fallback did) plus, for the fallback path, a price_table_version (a sha256 content hash of the canonical-json fallback price table) so the table that priced the call is identifiable; priced-at reuses created_at if that is already the call-time instant (justify in the story) else add a column. A NEW migration 0055 (revision '0055', down_revision '0054') adds them via batch_alter_table; it MUST NOT edit 0054 or any prior shipped migration; the single-head assertion (script.get_heads() == ['0055']) passes and downgrade drops exactly the added columns. A dedicated per-migration SQLite test (0051 pattern) applies 0055 and asserts the added llm_calls columns exist + are dropped by downgrade AND the LlmCall model declares them (the default gate builds the DB from models, so this per-migration test is the only default-gate check that the migration matches the model).

At the persist boundary (game_runner _priced_cost_usd / _persist_llm_call) the pricing basis that produced cost_usd is stamped on the row: a provider-priced call (response_cost present - the PRIMARY path, human_cost_governance.price_turn_usd returns response_cost directly) stamps price_basis='PROVIDER_RESPONSE_COST' (cost_usd is already immutable - stored verbatim from the provider, never recomputed); a fallback-priced call (response_cost None) stamps price_basis='FALLBACK_TABLE' + the fallback table content hash; a test asserts each path stamps the correct basis/version.

Immutability: a test changes the fallback price table AFTER a call is persisted and asserts the historical LlmCall.cost_usd AND its stamped version are unchanged (the new rate does not rewrite history); a NEW call after the change carries the NEW version.
Campaign/spend reporting (US-268) can attribute spend to a price-table version from the row alone; a test asserts spend grouped by stamped version is recoverable.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Sites: db/models.LlmCall (~280-331: cost_usd, tokens, model, NO price_version today), game_runner _priced_cost_usd (~606-623) / _persist_llm_call (~626-653), economics/human_cost_governance.price_turn_usd + settings.padrino_human_fallback_token_price_per_1k. Provider-invoice reconciliation is OUT of scope (derived cost is sufficient; just make it immutable). NEW migration 0055 (down_revision '0054'); US-266 depends on US-263. NOTE: the PRIMARY pricing path is LiteLLM response_cost (price_turn_usd returns it directly, ~110-111); the fallback token-price table is consulted only when response_cost is None - so price_basis distinguishes the two and 'immutable across a fallback-rate change' is most load-bearing for the fallback subset (provider-priced cost_usd is already immutable, stored verbatim).

userStories.title: Budget-burn + cost-drift Prometheus gauges + alert keys
userStories.description: Operators need to SEE budget burn and cost drift while a campaign runs. Add Prometheus gauges (observability/metrics.py module-level singletons on the shared REGISTRY) for budget burn (global + per-campaign spend vs cap, fraction consumed) and a cost-drift signal (e.g. observed per-call cost vs the stamped price-table expectation, or fallback-priced fraction). Add alert keys (observability/alerts.py constants + AlertNotifier.fire/resolve) for budget-burn (cap nearing/reached) and cost-drift (derived cost diverging beyond a threshold). Follow the existing pattern: module-level metric singletons + record_* helpers; ALERT_* constants in ALERT_KEYS + notifier.fire(key, **details) on breach / notifier.resolve(key) on recovery.
acceptanceCriteria: observability/metrics.py gains budget-burn gauges (global spend, per-campaign spend, fraction-of-cap) registered on the shared REGISTRY with appropriate labels, plus a cost-drift gauge/metric; a test asserts the gauges are registered and a record_* helper updates them.

observability/alerts.py gains a budget-burn alert key and a cost-drift alert key in ALERT_KEYS; a test asserts notifier.fire(BUDGET_BURN_KEY) flips it active and notifier.resolve clears it (the existing fire/resolve transition de-dup pattern).
The budget-burn alert fires when spend crosses a settings-driven threshold of the cap (e.g. 80%/100%) and resolves when it recovers; a test asserts the threshold gating with an injected spend/cap.

The cost-drift alert fires when derived per-call cost diverges beyond a settings-driven threshold (e.g. a spike in fallback-priced fraction or a per-call cost far above the stamped expectation); a test asserts the drift threshold gating.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Pattern: observability/metrics.py module-level Counter/Histogram/Gauge on REGISTRY + record_* helpers; observability/alerts.py ALERT_* constants in ALERT_KEYS + AlertNotifier.fire/resolve (existing keys: spend.cap.reached, scheduler.heartbeat.stale, admission.denied.streak). Reuse spend.cap.reached if it already fits, else add a campaign-scoped budget-burn key. Thresholds from settings. Stamped price-table version from US-266 feeds cost-drift.

userStories.title: Campaign report: cost, progress, ETA, per-scope convergence (CLI + JSON)
userStories.description: Surface a Campaign report via CLI/JSON (no frontend campaign UI this wave - CLI/API first, frontend is out of scope). The report aggregates, for a campaign: total cost (from immutably-stamped LlmCall costs, US-266, attributable to the campaign's games), progress ('X of N cells done', materialized/completed/dead-letter counts), a simple ETA (remaining cells * observed per-cell duration/cost), and PER-SCOPE convergence: for each scope (per-ruleset / per-faction / per-model) the games count, sigma, provisional flag, and rank-stability over the last K rating updates. The documented DEAD_LETTER holes (US-260) are listed. This is the operator's single view of a running/finished campaign and the data source the publish gate (US-269) consumes.
acceptanceCriteria: A campaign report function returns a typed/JSON structure with: total cost (summed from the campaign's games' stamped LlmCall costs), progress (X of N cells: PENDING/MATERIALIZED/COMPLETED/DEAD_LETTER counts), an ETA estimate (remaining cells * observed per-cell cost/duration), and a list of DEAD_LETTER holes with their last_error; a test asserts each field for a fixture campaign.

Per-scope convergence is included: per scope (ruleset/faction/model as available from ratings) the games count, sigma, provisional flag, and a rank-stability measure over the last K updates (K from the campaign's rank_stability_k); a test asserts convergence fields are populated from rating/rollup data.
A CLI command exposes the report as JSON (and a human-readable summary); a test invokes the CLI/report function and asserts valid JSON with the documented shape (no frontend - CLI/API only).

Cost is attributed via the immutable stamped price (US-266) so re-running the report after a rate change yields the same historical cost; a test asserts report cost is stable across a fallback-rate change.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Convergence inputs: ratings/model_rollup (sigma, games, conservative_score) + ratings/public_leaderboard (provisional, rank, sample_count). Cost from stamped LlmCall (US-266). Progress from campaign_pairings cell statuses (US-258/261). DEAD_LETTER holes from US-260. rank_stability_k from the campaign row (US-258). NO frontend (out of scope). This feeds US-269.

userStories.title: Uncertainty-based publish-gate checklist (ready-to-publish boolean + blockers list)
userStories.description: The publish gate is UNCERTAINTY-based and auto-finalizing: a scope is publishable when per-scope games >= target AND sigma <= target AND non-provisional AND rank-stable over the last K updates; DEAD_LETTER holes are documented (not blockers to finalize). Add a publish-gate checklist that returns a 'ready to publish' boolean + an itemized blockers list, checking: (1) replay-hash verify (the campaign's games re-verify their hash chains), (2) privacy/anonymity audit (no identity leak on any published surface - reuse the FORBIDDEN_PAYLOAD_KEYS / identity-blind guards), (3) FAILED/dead-letter exclusion (FAILED games and DEAD_LETTER cells are excluded from published ratings - they never reached a valid terminal_result so _should_apply_ratings already excludes FAILED), (4) min-coverage per model (per-model games >= target), (5) sigma <= target AND rank-stable over last K, (6) derived-cost present/immutable (every published game's LlmCalls carry stamped immutable cost, US-266). The CANONICAL firewall is respected: only CANONICAL_TEAM mirror results feed the canonical ladder; non-canonical/PLACEMENT results never reach it. AUTO-FINALIZE: the gate informs publishability but does NOT hard-block campaign finalization (the campaign auto-finalizes; the gate gates PUBLICATION, not finalization).
acceptanceCriteria: A publish-gate function returns (ready_to_publish: bool, blockers: list[...]) evaluating: replay-hash verify across the campaign's games, identity/anonymity audit (no FORBIDDEN_PAYLOAD_KEYS / no identity leak on the published surface), FAILED+DEAD_LETTER exclusion from published ratings, per-model min-coverage >= target, sigma <= target AND rank-stable over last K, and derived-cost present+immutable; a test asserts ready_to_publish is True only when ALL pass and False with the specific blocker(s) listed otherwise.

Each failing check appears as an itemized blocker (e.g. 'model X under-sampled: 32 < 50', 'scope Y sigma 3.1 > target 2.5', 'cell Z dead-lettered'); a test asserts the blocker list names the specific failing scope/model.

FAILED games and DEAD_LETTER cells are excluded from the published ratings/coverage counts (they never reached a valid terminal_result; _should_apply_ratings already excludes FAILED); a test asserts a campaign with a DEAD_LETTER cell can still be ready_to_publish (the hole is documented, not a blocker) when all surviving scopes meet target.

CANONICAL firewall respected: the gate's canonical-ladder publishability only considers CANONICAL_TEAM mirror results; a test asserts a non-canonical/PLACEMENT result is never counted toward canonical publishability.

AUTO-FINALIZE preserved: the gate does NOT block campaign finalization (the campaign auto-finalizes per US-261); the gate only reports publication readiness; a test asserts a finalized-but-not-yet-publishable campaign returns ready_to_publish=False without having blocked finalization.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Uncertainty-based publish criteria (locked decision): games>=target AND sigma<=target AND non-provisional AND rank-stable over last K. Inputs: report (US-268), ratings provisional/sigma/rank, hash-chain verify (replay_event_log), anonymity guards (FORBIDDEN_PAYLOAD_KEYS / identity-blind), _should_apply_ratings (FAILED excluded), CANONICAL_TEAM firewall (RatingContextKind.CANONICAL_TEAM, public_leaderboard ~550/739/785). DEAD_LETTER holes are documented, not blockers. The gate gates PUBLICATION, not finalization (auto-finalize stays).
